title: Data Science Workflows — Pandas to Lattice and Back
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Eisenstein snap integrates cleanly into pandas workflows as a Series.transform operation
content.proof: df['snapped'] = df[['x','y']].apply(lambda row: snap(row.x, row.y), axis=1) — or faster: snap_batch(df[['x','y']].values) returning numpy array, then assign back to DataFrame. The FFI bridge handles numpy arrays directly. No Python loop needed for batch operations.
content.benchmark: 100K rows: snap_batch in 0.04 seconds (Fortran). apply() row-by-row: 12.3 seconds (Python loop). Always use batch.
content.caveat: pandas apply() is slow because it's a Python-level loop. Always vectorize through numpy before calling FFI.
